title: Hijri Calendar for Any Country, State or City in the World
excerpt: Access the accurate Islamic Hijri calendar for any city worldwide — from Karachi to London, New York to Kuala Lumpur. Understand why moon-sighting differences exist and how to find the correct dates for your location.
author: QuranBookk
content:
## Why Two Muslims in the Same Family Can Be Observing Different Islamic Dates

A Pakistani family in London calls their relatives in Karachi on the evening of the 29th of Sha'ban. The family in London has just made their moon-sighting declaration: Ramadan begins tomorrow. Their relatives in Karachi respond: we are still in Sha'ban — we will start the day after.

This is not a dispute. Both families are right, according to their respective scholarly traditions. And it happens every year, across dozens of Islamic dates, in Muslim communities around the world.

The [Global Hijri Calendars directory](/islamic-calendar/worldwide/) exists to make sense of these differences — and to give every Muslim the correct Hijri calendar for their specific country, state, or city.

## Why the Same Islamic Date Differs Between Countries

The Hijri calendar is a **lunar calendar** — each month begins with the confirmed sighting of the new moon crescent. Unlike the Gregorian calendar, which is calculated centuries in advance, each Hijri month begins when the moon is actually seen.

Three scholarly approaches exist for moon sighting:

**1. Global Sighting** — If the moon is sighted anywhere in the world, the new month begins for all Muslims globally on the same day. Saudi Arabia and several Gulf countries generally follow this approach.

**2. Regional/Local Sighting** — The new month begins only when the moon is sighted locally (within the same geographical region or country). Pakistan, India, Bangladesh, and Morocco historically follow this approach, which means their Islamic dates may differ from Saudi Arabia by one day.

**3. Astronomical Calculation** — The month begins based on pre-calculated astronomical data (when the new moon conjunction occurs and the moon becomes theoretically visible). Several Western countries and Turkey follow this approach for practical predictability.

The result: on any given Islamic date, a Muslim in Riyadh, one in Karachi, and one in Morocco may each be observing a different Gregorian date as the same Islamic date — and each is following a legitimate scholarly position.

## What the Global Hijri Calendars Directory Provides

The [Global Hijri Calendars directory](/islamic-calendar/worldwide/) is organized by **Country → State/Province → City**, giving each location its own calendar that reflects:

- The Hijri dates as observed in that country/region
- The corresponding Gregorian dates
- Prayer times integrated for that city's coordinates
- Major Islamic events marked per the regional observance

This means a user in London gets the Islamic calendar as observed by UK moon-sighting authorities, while a user in Karachi gets the calendar as observed in Pakistan — not a generic global calendar that may be a day off for either of them.

## Eight Uses for the Worldwide Hijri Calendar Directory

1. **Diaspora Muslims** confirming Islamic dates for their country of heritage vs. their country of residence
2. **Families spread across continents** coordinating Eid calls and gifts with the right date for each family member's location
3. **Islamic content creators** publishing content with the correct dates for their international audience
4. **Mosque and Islamic centre administrators** who serve communities from multiple countries of origin
5. **Muslim travellers** knowing which Islamic dates fall during their planned travel period
6. **New Muslims** trying to understand why Islamic dates differ between countries
7. **Non-Muslim researchers and journalists** covering Islamic observances needing country-specific dates
8. **Teachers and educators** explaining the Islamic calendar to students with family in different countries

<Callout type="tip">
During Ramadan, the most practically important difference is the Fajr time — which determines when Suhoor must end. City-level pages in the directory use the precise geographic coordinates of each city, giving a more accurate Fajr time than a country-level approximation.
</Callout>

## Pairing the Global Calendar with Other Tools

The Global Hijri Calendars directory complements the [Islamic Hijri Calendar](/islamic-calendar/) — the GPS-based calendar for your current location. Use the worldwide directory for planning and reference, and the live calendar for day-to-day use.

For the prayer times at a specific destination city (whether or not you need the calendar), the [Global Prayer Times directory](/prayer-times/worldwide/) provides the same geographic depth with daily-updated prayer times for every city.

## The Hijri Calendar, Globally Accurate

The [Global Hijri Calendars directory](/islamic-calendar/worldwide/) is free, requires no account, and is accessible from any browser. Whether you need the Hijri calendar for Karachi, London, New York, or Kuala Lumpur — every city, every region, every country — the correct calendar for that location is one search away.

👉 [Find the Hijri calendar for any city worldwide](/islamic-calendar/worldwide/)
